title: leionema gracile
intro: Leionema gracile, also known as the Gracile Leionema or Gracile Leionema Bitter-pea, is a plant species that belongs to the Rutaceae family of flowering plants. It is native to eastern Australia, where it grows as a shrub or small tree with small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Leionema gracile can be prepared in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
